content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке
Document lines: 619-628

## 8. Модель данных в одной строке

- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено
- `Entity` = кто/что это вообще
- `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт
- `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом
- `Profile` = компактная проекция устойчивого контекста
- `Task` = что должно быть сделано
- `Episode` = чему система научилась
- `MemoryRun` = как именно память обновлялась

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=v3 one-line role map for core memory objects summary=Section 8 defines a compact mnemonic mapping of each v3 memory object to its role: Source (observed/said input), Entity (what/who it is), Note (important context not yet hardened), Claim (normalized fact), Profile (compact stable-context projection), Task (to be done), Episode (what the system learned), and MemoryRun (how memory was updated). scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 8. Модель данных в одной строке' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0> keywords=v3,Source,Entity,Note,Claim,Profile,Task,Episode,MemoryRun tags=data_model,doc_digest,role_map evidence=- `Source` = что было сказано / увидено - `Entity` = кто/что это вообще - `Note` = что уже важно и полезно, но еще не хочется цементировать как факт - `Claim` = что мы считаем нормализованным фактом - `Profile` = компакт...[truncated 152 chars]

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 memory model layers and design goals summary=The v3 data model is designed to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability by splitting memory into layers: Source (evidence), Entity/Claim (semantic graph), Note (intermediate), Profile/Task/Episode (operational projections), and MemoryRun (audit). scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3 data model,memory layers,typed memory,lineage,temporal truth tags=data_model,architecture,memory_v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Note` entity role, boundaries, and invariants in v3 summary=`Note` is an intermediate semantic memory fragment used for important retrieval-useful context that should not yet be hardened into final claims; it excludes source records and chain-of-thought, and follows invariants like one semantic fragment per note and self-contained meaning. scope=Applies to section 3.4 `Note` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md) keywords=Note,intermediate memory,retrieval,invariants,candidate_claims_json tags=data_model,note_semantics,v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Entity` as canonical memory node with alias/merge semantics summary=In section 3.2, `Entity` is the canonical memory node that unifies mentions of the same object and anchors claims/tasks/notes/episodes; it uses aliases and merge status while preserving history, keeping aliases pointing to the current canonical entity, and allowing erroneous merges to be reversed via audit trail. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,memory_v3,entity_component useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=STABILIZING title=Cross-cutting policy: scope, retrieval lifecycle, telemetry, and retention summary=In v3, scope and retrieval lifecycle are explicit cross-cutting concerns: use dual scope fields (`scope_text` for explainability and `scope_json` for retrieval/reconciliation), keep `truth status` separate from `retrieval_state`, apply retrieval states active/warm/cold/archived/deleted, track usage fields (`use_count`, `last_used_at`, `last_validated_at`), and enforce explicit retention policy: lo...[truncated 320 chars] scope=Applies to cross-cutting fields and retention policy in '02. Data Model / 1..2 and 6' of agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=scope_text,scope_json,retrieval_state,truth status,usage fields tags=cross_cutting,retrieval_lifecycle,schema_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`MemoryRun` schema guidance and invariants (`run log` retention, `input_hash` idempotency) summary=Section 3.9 provides recommended `MemoryRun` fields (identity, run metadata, retrieval inputs, prompt/model versions, outputs, applied/repair ops, latency/tokens, status/errors, timestamp) and defines invariants: run logs must not disappear silently, and `input_hash` supports idempotency and retry safety. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.9 `MemoryRun` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=MemoryRun fields,run_type,trigger_mode,input_hash,idempotency tags=schema_guidance,invariants,memoryrun useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<5> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim and Note serve complementary roles in memory summary=The section defines a deliberate split: `Claim` is for precise, reconcilable, time-aware factual usage, while `Note` is for richer contextual meaning (decision history, rationale, evolving meaning) with later consolidation. Overusing only one type causes imbalance: false precision if everything is a `Claim`, or overly soft memory if everything is a `Note`. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 4. Почему `Note` не заменяет `Claim` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Claim vs Note roles,false precision,soft memory tags=data_model,memory_design,claim_note_boundary useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<6> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`PredicateCatalog` is a reconciliation config, not a memory item summary=In the v3 data model, `PredicateCatalog` is defined as configuration for reconciliation logic rather than a stored memory item; it encodes per-predicate rules such as cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and projection sync flags. scope=Applies to section 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` in 02. Data Model (agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md) keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy tags=data_model,reconciliation_config,v3 useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<7> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Profile` is a compact, rebuildable projection layer summary=In section 3.6, `Profile` is defined as an always-in-context projection, not a source of truth, built from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context; it should stay short, prioritize explicit facts, keep disputable facts in claim/note, and remain rebuildable. scope=Applies to 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.6 `Profile` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Profile,projection layer,always-in-context,rebuildable tags=data_model,profile,doc_digest,v3 useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

structured.fullText: {"operations":[{"action":"noop","candidate_index":0,"links_to_create":[],"patch":null,"reason":"Candidate is a concise restatement of the same role map/layering already captured in the existing v3 memory model digest; no distinct reusable semantic fragment added.","target_note_id":"hot-path:note:<0>"}]}
